info.title: Vitorio.CLI
info.description: CLI based on useful tool for developers. All data generated and manipulated by it are the total responsibility of those who use it;

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: CLI with some tolls to make your developer life easier (or not)

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

commands.name: about
commands.description: About this CLI

commands.name: gen
commands.description: Generates some kind of data

commands.name: cpf
commands.description: Generates valid CPF

options.name: --formatted
options.description: Generate CPF with punctuation [default: False]

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of CPFs to be generated [default: 1]

commands.name: cnpj
commands.description: Generates valid CNPJ

options.name: --formatted
options.description: Generate CNPJ with punctuation [default: False]

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of CNPJs to be generated [default: 1]

commands.name: guid
commands.description: Generate GUIDs

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of GUIDs to generate [default: 1]

commands.name: email
commands.description: Generates email address (not necessarily valid)

options.name: --provider
options.description: Email provider (e.g.: gmail, yahoo, outlook, etc.)

options.name: --domain
options.description: Email domain (e.g.: com, com.br, etc.) [default: com]

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of emails to be generated [default: 1]

commands.name: phone
commands.description: Generate phone number

options.name: --code-country
options.description: Country code [default: 0]

options.name: --ddd
options.description: DDD code of the destination location [default: 0]

options.name: --number-of-digits
options.description: Number of digits in the number (Min = 3, Max = 9) [default: 9]

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of telephone numbers to be generated [default: 1]

options.name: --not-formatted
options.description: Do not format the phone number [default: False]

commands.name: password
commands.description: Generate password with random characters

options.name: --length
options.description: Length of the password (Min: 3, Max: 16) [default: 8]

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of passwords to be generated [default: 1]

commands.name: name
commands.description: Generate random name

options.name: --gender
options.description: Generate famale (F), male (M) or all (A) names [default: A]

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of names to be generated [default: 1]

commands.name: cep
commands.description: Generates CEPs (valid or not)

options.name: --formatted
options.description: Generates CEP with punctuation [default: False]

options.name: --count
options.description: Number of CEPs to be generated [default: 1]

commands.name: birthdate
commands.description: Generate birth date corresponding to an age

arguments.name: age
arguments.description: Age from which the birthday date will be get

commands.name: format
commands.description: Formats data using a mask

commands.name: cpf
commands.description: Formats a CPF with standard punctuation

options.name: --remove
options.description: Remove CPF formatting [default: False]

arguments.name: cpf
arguments.description: The CPF number to be formatted

commands.name: cnpj
commands.description: Formats a CNPJ with standard punctuation

options.name: --remove
options.description: Remove CNPJ formatting [default: False]

commands.name: date
commands.description: Format a date according to a mask

options.name: --mask
options.description: Mask for date formatting [default: dd/MM/yyyy hh:mm:ss]

options.name: --json
options.description: Format the date to JSON [default: False]

arguments.name: date
arguments.description: Date value to format ["now" to use the current system date, "utc" to use the current UTC date] [default: now]

commands.name: string
commands.description: Formats strings according to a pattern

options.name: --upper
options.description: Formats the string in uppercase [default: False]

options.name: --lower
options.description: Formats the string in lowercase [default: False]

commands.name: git-branch
commands.description: Takes a text input and formats it following the Git branch naming standard

options.name: --prefix
options.description: Prefix that will be added to the branch name separated by '/' [default: ]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: An input string to be formatted as a Git branch name

commands.name: list
commands.description: Formats the items in a list of strings according to a pattern

options.name: --separator
options.description:
The separator for each list item [default:
]

options.name: --prefix
options.description: Prefix to be placed on all items in the list [default: ]

options.name: --suffix
options.description: Suffix to be placed on all items in the list [default: ]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: A list of strings to be formatted

commands.name: list
commands.description: Formats the items in a list of strings according to a pattern

options.name: --separator
options.description:
The separator for each list item [default:
]

options.name: --prefix
options.description: Prefix to be placed on all items in the list [default: ]

options.name: --suffix
options.description: Suffix to be placed on all items in the list [default: ]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: A list of strings to be formatted

commands.name: guid
commands.description: Formats a GUID according to the specified format

options.name: --format
options.description: The format specifier (N, D, B, P or X) [default: D]

commands.name: convert
commands.description: Converts data from one type to another

commands.name: toBase64
commands.description: Converts an input to Base64 encoding

options.name: --file
options.description: Path to a file to be converted to Base64 [default: ]

options.name: --output
options.description: Specifies the path of a destination file for base64 output [default: ]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: The string to be converted to Base64 [default: ]

commands.name: fromBase64
commands.description: Converts an input to Base64 decoding

options.name: --file
options.description: Absolute path to base64 file (with file name and extension) [default: ]

options.name: --output
options.description: Specifies the path of a destination file for the decoded string output [default: ]

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: The input string to be decoded from Base64 [default: ]

commands.name: date
commands.description: Date operations

commands.name: difference
commands.description: Calculate the difference between two dates

arguments.name: start
arguments.description: The start date ["now" to use the current system date, "utc" to use the current UTC date]

arguments.name: end
arguments.description: The end date ["now" to use the current system date, "utc" to use the current UTC date]

commands.name: add
commands.description: Add a time to a date

arguments.name: start
arguments.description: The start date ["now" to use the current system date, "utc" to use the current UTC date]
